description: Pipe output to shell interpreter allows arbitrary command execution
remediation: Use specific commands instead of piping to a shell. Process output programmatically.

description: Backtick or $() command substitution can execute injected commands
remediation: Avoid dynamic command construction. Use parameterized commands or pre-validated inputs.

description: eval executes arbitrary string as shell command
remediation: Never use eval with dynamic input. Use direct command execution instead.

description: Subshell injection via variable expansion or command chaining
remediation: Validate and quote all variable expansions. Avoid uncontrolled command chaining.

description: Fork bomb pattern that exhausts system process table
remediation: Never execute recursive process spawning. Use controlled concurrency with limits.

description: Infinite loop pattern without exit condition may hang the system
remediation: Add explicit exit conditions and timeouts to all loops.

description: Creating extremely large files can exhaust disk space
remediation: Limit file creation size. Check available disk space before creating large files.

description: Python eval/exec via command line can execute arbitrary code
remediation: Write code to files and execute them directly instead of using eval/exec inline.

description: Dynamic import can load and execute arbitrary modules
remediation: Use static imports. If dynamic loading is needed, validate module names against an allowlist.

description: compile() with exec/eval mode enables arbitrary code execution
remediation: Avoid compile() with dynamic input. Use static code paths.

description: Inline code execution via interpreter flags
remediation: Write code to files and execute them. Avoid passing code as command-line strings.

description: HTTP POST to external host may exfiltrate data
remediation: Only send data to approved internal endpoints. Review all external HTTP POST requests.

description: Reverse shell pattern enables remote unauthorized access
remediation: Never establish reverse shell connections. Use approved remote access tools.

description: Port scanning can probe network infrastructure
remediation: Only scan explicitly authorized hosts. Document the scope and purpose.

description: Accessing system authentication files
remediation: Do not read system authentication files. Use proper user management APIs.

description: Accessing SSH keys and configuration
remediation: Never read or expose SSH keys. Use secure key management services.

description: Accessing environment files that may contain secrets
remediation: Use .env.example for templates. Access secrets via secure secret management.

description: Accessing private key files
remediation: Never read private key files directly. Use certificate management tools.

description: Privilege escalation via sudo or su
remediation: Operate within assigned permissions. Request elevated access through proper channels.

description: Setting world-writable permissions is a security risk
remediation: Use minimum required permissions. Typically 755 for directories, 644 for files.

description: Setting setuid/setgid bit enables privilege escalation
remediation: Never set setuid/setgid bits. Use proper capability management.

description: Base64 decode piped to shell hides malicious commands
remediation: Never pipe decoded content to a shell. Inspect decoded content before execution.

description: Manipulating shell history to hide activity
remediation: Preserve shell history for audit trails. Do not clear or disable history.

description: Overriding common command aliases can mask malicious behavior
remediation: Do not override standard command aliases. Use unique names for custom functions.

description: Recursive force delete can destroy entire filesystems
remediation: Use targeted rm on specific files. Never use rm -rf on broad paths.

description: Disk formatting or raw device write can destroy data
remediation: Never format disks or write raw data to devices.

description: SQL destructive operations can cause irreversible data loss
remediation: Use targeted DELETE with WHERE clause. Never DROP or TRUNCATE without backup.

description: Destructive git operations can cause irreversible work loss
remediation: Use git push --force-with-lease. Stash before reset.
